title: ERPNext
description: Open-source ERP suite (accounting, inventory, HR, CRM, manufacturing, projects) built on the Frappe framework.
installation: ERPNext is a MULTI-CONTAINER Frappe stack. This template deploys the PRIMARY backend (gunicorn) image frappe/erpnext, which serves the Frappe HTTP API/app on port 8000. To run a complete, production-ready install you MUST deploy these companion services from the SAME frappe/erpnext image (only the command differs) plus the nginx frontend, and run one-time site creation: (1) frontend - image frappe/erpnext, command 'nginx-entrypoint.sh', env BACKEND=<this-app>:8000, SOCKETIO=<websocket>:9000, FRAPPE_SITE_NAME_HEADER, UPSTREAM_REAL_IP_ADDRESS - this is the public web server (port 8080). (2) websocket - image frappe/erpnext, command 'node /home/frappe/frappe-bench/apps/frappe/socketio.js' (port 9000, real-time). (3) queue-short - command 'bench worker --queue short,default'. (4) queue-long - command 'bench worker --queue long,default,short'. (5) scheduler - command 'bench schedule'. (6) configurator (run-once init) - command writes common_site_config.json from DB_HOST/DB_PORT/REDIS_CACHE/REDIS_QUEUE/SOCKETIO_PORT then exits. (7) create-site (run-once Job) - 'bench new-site' with --db-type mariadb, --mariadb-root-password, --admin-password, then 'bench --site <site> install-app erpnext' and 'bench use <site>'. All services share the /home/frappe/frappe-bench/sites volume (the extraVolume below). FIRST RUN: rotate the MariaDB password (default 'erpnext'), set a strong Administrator password during create-site, then log in at the app URL as user 'Administrator'. SEARCH (optional): full-text search uses the built-in DB by default; ERPNext can optionally integrate an external search backend - omitted here. Frappe officially supports MariaDB (first-class) and PostgreSQL; this template uses the MariaDB add-on as the canonical/recommended database.
